rect.data-narration: This section explains the concept of global states in distributed systems.
In distributed systems, a global state refers to the current status or configuration of the system as a whole.

g.data-narration: In a distributed system, clocks are used to synchronize the timing of different nodes and processes.

circle.data-narration: Local Clocks: Each node has its own local clock that keeps track of time.

line.data-narration: Global Clocks: A global clock is a synchronized clock that represents the current time across all nodes in the system.

g.data-narration: In a distributed system, events are the occurrences or changes that happen in the system, while process states refer to the current status of a process.

rect.data-narration: Event Types: There are two main types of events: Internal Events and External Events.
Process States: A process can be in one of three states: Ready State, Running State, and Waiting State.

g.data-narration: A global state is defined as the current configuration of the system, consisting of the states of all processes, threads, and nodes.

rect.data-narration: Global State Variables: Global state variables are variables that represent the current state of the system, such as the number of processes, threads, and nodes.

line.data-narration: Global State Transition Function: The global state transition function determines the next state of the system based on the current state and the events that occur.

rect.data-narration: Global State: The global state is the current configuration of the system, taking into account the states of all processes, threads, and nodes.

g.data-narration: Arrow markers are used to connect the different sections and illustrate the relationships between them.
In a distributed system, events are the occurrences or changes that happen in the system, while process states refer to the current status of a process.

rect.data-narration: Event Types: There are two main types of events: Internal Events and External Events.
Process States: A process can be in one of three states: Ready State, Running State, and Waiting State.
